preamble: The shift from the high front vowel [i] to the mid front vowel [e] is a process of Vowel Lowering. This is most characteristic of the evolution of the Romance languages from Latin, where short [i] lowered to mid-vowel positions while long [i:] remained high.

examples.note: Latin short /i/ lowered to /e/ in most Romance languages.
The Latin short high vowel became a mid vowel.

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction.
Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
